channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Modernising Agriculture to Boost Tanzania’s Economy and Livelihoods
item.description: Tanzania is moving forward with its National Development Vision 2050, aiming to create a strong, inclusive middle-income economy. Agriculture, which remains crucial for millions of Tanzanians, especia...

item.title: The U.K. House of Lords Ends Hereditary Peer Representation
item.description: On March 14, 2026, the United Kingdom took a significant step in parliamentary reform by voting to remove the last remaining hereditary peers from the House of Lords, marking the end of nearly a mille...

item.title: Aid Cuts Strain Sierra Leone's Busiest Maternity Hospital Operations
item.description: At Sierra Leona’s Princess Christian Maternity Hospital (PCMH), the country’s busiest maternity facility in Freetown, things are tough as foreign aid cuts hit hard. Nurses and doctors are working unde...

item.title: Government prepares contingency plans for rising energy costs amid Middle East conflict
item.description: The UK government is closely monitoring the potential economic fallout from the ongoing conflict in Iran, with particular concern about surging energy prices. Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has empha...

item.title: Cambridge researchers develop light-driven reaction to efficiently modify drug molecules
item.description: Scientists from the University of Cambridge have discovered a light-powered chemical reaction that could revolutionize how drugs are developed and modified. The unexpected finding, published in Nature...

item.title: Deputy President Mashatile Pays Tribute to Mosiuoa Lekota's Lifelong Commitment to Freedom and Justice
item.description: Mourners gathered in Bloemfontein to pay tribute to Mosiuoa Lekota, a stalwart of South Africa’s liberation struggle. Deputy President Paul Mashatile delivered a heartfelt eulogy, describing Lekota as...

item.title: Yhemo Lee Gifts His Mother Another Car on International Women's Day
item.description: Nigerian entertainer Yhemo Lee, wey people sabi for music and Lagos nightlife, don make headline again as e gift him mama another car. Dis na the fourth car wey he don buy for her, and e cost over six...

item.title: Cebu Bus Rapid Transit Now Operational After Long Delays
item.description: The Cebu Bus Rapid Transit (CBRT) system officially began its operations on Friday morning, March 13, 2026, marking a major milestone for Cebu City’s public transport modernization plan. The launch co...

item.title: Challenges and Opportunities in Tanzania's Natural Gas Sector
item.description: A new study warns say Tanzania fit struggle to get full benefits from its big natural gas reserves unless governance and institutional capacity improve. The report, 'Exploring the Nexus Between Oil an...

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk released from Jodhpur jail after NSA detention
item.description: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk was released from the central jail in Jodhpur, Rajasthan on Saturday, six months after being detained under the National Security Act (NSA). His detention followed viol...

item.title: Man jailed for stalking ex-partner and luring strangers to her home for sex
item.description: A 24-year-old woman, referred to as Kimberley to protect her identity, endured nearly a decade of online harassment and stalking by her former partner, Declan Guess. The pair first met at school when ...

item.title: Harris Okonkwo Endorses Peter Obi As ADC Presidential Candidate
item.description: As politicking dey hot ahead of 2027 elections, Hon. Harris Okonkwo, wey dey represent Idemili North and South for House of Representatives, don show say e dey confident say former Anambra State gover...

item.title: China Calls for Dialogue Between Pakistan and Afghanistan to Ease Border Tensions
item.description: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi has called on both Pakistan and Afghanistan to remain calm and resolve their ongoing disputes through dialogue rather than military action. The statement came after Pa...

item.title: Mark Carney’s First Year as Prime Minister: Navigating Crises and Politics
item.description: Mark Carney’s first year as Canada’s Prime Minister has been defined by his crisis leadership and unexpected political skill. Initially unknown to 30 per cent of Canadians, Carney rose to prominence a...

item.title: Western Cape Accelerates Foot and Mouth Disease Vaccination Campaign with 100 000 New Doses
item.description: The Western Cape Government has received a shipment of 100 000 Foot and Mouth Disease (FMD) vaccine doses at Cape Town International Airport, boosting efforts to safeguard the province's livestock sec...

item.title: Iran Launches Multiple Missiles at UAE; Six Dead, 141 Injured from Various Countries
item.description: On 14th March 2026, United Arab Emirates dey report say dem air defence systems don intercept nine ballistic missiles and thirty-three UAVs wey Iran launch. This na part of ongoing tensions wey dey be...

item.title: Kyle Flanagan sustains ankle injury during Storm clash
item.description: Dragons playmaker Kyle Flanagan suffered an ankle injury during a recent NRL clash against the Melbourne Storm. The incident occurred while Flanagan was tackled, leaving the team concerned about his f...

item.title: President Ruto Marks 100 Years of Kapsabet High, Announces Record Education Funding
item.description: President William Ruto revisited his alma mater, Kapsabet High School, on Thursday to celebrate the school's centenary. He described the visit as a personal homecoming, reflecting on the discipline, h...

item.title: Kenya to Streamline Healthcare Access for Teachers Through SHA Following KNUT Talks
item.description: The Kenyan government is stepping up efforts to make healthcare more accessible for teachers by transitioning them to the Social Health Authority (SHA). Health CS Aden Duale met with Kenya National Un...

item.title: US Strikes Iranian Military Targets on Kharg Island Amid Escalating Regional Conflict
item.description: The US has carried out military strikes on Iran’s Kharg Island, a strategic hub handling about 90% of the country’s crude oil exports, targeting over 90 military facilities while leaving oil infrastru...
